data.title: Graco Board Member Brett Carter Resigns
data.description:
By Elias Schisgall

Graco board member Brett Carter is stepping down effective immediately.

Carter notified the board of his resignation on Thursday, the industrial manufacturing company disclosed in a Friday filing with the Securities and Exchange Commission.

He left the board to more fully pursue other commitments, and his departure was not due to any disagreement with the board or the company, Graco said.

Carter was on the board's audit committee and the management organization and compensation committee. His term was set to expire during the 2027 annual shareholder meeting.

Write to Elias Schisgall at elias.schisgall@wsj.com

data.title: Toyota Deploys 200 Staff to Support Joby as Shares Fall 23%
data.description:
Toyota Motor Corp. is intensifying its operational involvement with Joby Aviation as it works to protect its nearly $1 billion investment pledge, shifting from capital provider toward manufacturing partner. After becoming Joby's largest shareholder last year, Toyota has deployed nearly 200 employees to support the air taxi developer with critical parts, production guidance and assembly expertise anchored in the Toyota Production System. Sandy Lobenstein, Toyota's group vice president of flying mobility, indicated the effort is focused on embedding TPS principles directly into Joby's operations, with the goal of improving lead times and supporting a future production ramp.

Joby remains in the pre-commercial phase, producing roughly one aircraft per month at its California facility while targeting an increase to as many as four per month by 2027. Over the longer term, the company has said it aims to reach up to 500 aircraft annually once facilities in California and Ohio are fully built out. Full certification from the US Federal Aviation Administration and other regulators is still pending, though Chief Product Officer Eric Allison said the company is targeting the start of passenger flights this year. Earlier service targets of 2024 and 2025 have been pushed back, and while Dubai had previously been cited as an early launch market, the exact timing remains unclear.

As certification advances and the final $250 million tranche of Toyota's pledged $894 million funding remains outstanding, Joby is assembling prototype aircraft and stress-testing components and manufacturing processes. Management said non-conforming parts are trending in the right direction, without disclosing specific defect rates. Shares are down about 23% this year, pressured in part after the company announced a $1 billion equity and convertible bond offering, though the stock rose 2.9% to $10.16 in New York trading on Friday morning. Toyota is also considering a proposed strategic manufacturing alliance, which could potentially deepen the relationship further as Joby moves toward higher production volumes.

data.title: Bitcoin bulls blitz $69K as retail traders pressure short positioning
data.description:
Bitcoin (BTC) rallied to $69,482 on Friday, and the rally coincided with data showing steady accumulation from smaller-sized holders in February.

Analysts say the breakout may evolve into a broader bullish trend, although other data suggest that a longer period of price consolidation will underlie the emerging bull trend.

Key takeaways:

  • BTC broke above the $69,000 resistance and its descending channel, triggering $92 million in short liquidations within four hours.

  • Small wallets added $613 million in February, while the whale wallets stalled with $4.5B billion in outflows.

  • Short-term holder profit-ratio indicator hit its lowest level since November 2022, underscoring weak sentiment over the past few weeks. 

Will the Bitcoin relief rally last?

Bitcoin has pushed above the upper boundary of its descending channel and retested $69,000. The move marks a potential bullish break of structure (BOS), if BTC holds above $68,000.

If BTC holds above this reclaimed level, the next internal liquidity zones sit near $71,500 and $74,000. The 50 and 100-period exponential moving averages (EMAs) are now compressing beneath the price on the one-hour chart, reinforcing the possibility of the short-term momentum continuing.

The latest price surge triggered roughly $96 million in futures liquidations over the past four hours, with nearly $92 million coming from short positions, signaling a short squeeze on bearish traders.

BTC liquidations were primarily concentrated on Bybit (22.5%), Hyperliquid (22%), and Gate (15%), suggesting these platforms account for a significant share of active leveraged positioning in the market.

Related: Multi-day negative Bitcoin funding signals ‘overcrowded’ short trade: Reversal coming?

BTC retail investor demand backs the breakout

The breakout is supported by the steady buying from the smaller-sized investors. Order flow data from Hyblock shows that the small wallets ($0–$10,000) have accumulated roughly $613 million in cumulative volume delta (CVD) in February, consistently bidding during the price correction.

The mid-sized wallets ($10,000–$100,000) remain around -$216 million for the month, but the cohort added roughly $300 million since BTC fell below $60,000, suggesting selective accumulation during discounted periods.

Whale wallets ($100,000 and above) saw their CVD bottom near -$5.8 billion earlier in February and have since moved sideways. This stabilization implies that the aggressive distribution has paused, though a clear accumulation trend from the large holders has yet to emerge.

For the rally to continue, whale buying may need to return, and the short-term holder spent output profit ratio (SOPR) may need to move back above 1, signaling that the recent buyers are no longer selling at a loss.

Notably, the short-term holder SOPR recently fell to its lowest level since November 2022, indicating that many recent buyers have been realizing losses, a sign that conviction may remain fragile despite the rebound.

Related: Bitcoin passes $69K on slower US CPI print, but Fed rate-cut odds stay low

data.title: Walmart Seen Entering 2026 With Momentum — Market Talk
data.description: Walmart is set up well for 2026, though it will likely offer measured guidance resulting in a below-consensus initial FY outlook, say Deutsche Bank analysts. The company's first half will benefit from a strong tax return season, which should boost its general merchandise business, while its scale and profit diversity will allow it to mitigate tariff and inflationary pressures. Walmart is now "closing its chapter of building out the foundation for its digital capabilities, including AI, and is shifting gears into acceleration mode," the analysts say. However, risks in the consumer backdrop remain given high interest rates, a stretched middle-income consumer and above-average layoffs. Walmart is slated to report 4Q results on February 19. (nicholas.miller@wsj.com)

data.title: WEX Inc. SEC 10-K Report
data.description:
WEX Inc., a leading provider of payment processing and information management services, has released its 2025 10-K report, detailing the company's financial and operational performance over the past year. The report highlights key financial metrics, business achievements, strategic initiatives, and the challenges and risks faced by the company.

Financial Highlights

  • Total Revenues: $2,660.8 million, reflecting a slight increase from $2,628.1 million in 2024, driven by growth in account servicing and other revenue.
  • Operating Income: $663.9 million, a decrease from $686.3 million in 2024, primarily due to increased sales and marketing expenses.
  • Net Income Attributable to Shareholders: $304.1 million, slightly down from $309.6 million in 2024, impacted by higher income tax provision.
  • Diluted EPS: $8.47, compared to $7.50 in 2024, indicating improved earnings per share despite a decrease in net income.
  • Total Cost of Services: $1,100.6 million, up from $1,037.8 million in 2024, indicating increased costs.

Business Highlights

  • Revenue Segments: WEX operates through three main business segments: Mobility, Benefits, and Corporate Payments. The Mobility segment focuses on fleet payment solutions and transaction processing, the Benefits segment provides SaaS software for employee benefits administration, and the Corporate Payments segment offers global B2B payment solutions.
  • Geographical Performance: The Mobility segment serves diverse fleet needs globally, with specific business units addressing North American Mobility, Over-the-Road, and International Mobility, including Europe and Asia-Pacific. The proprietary closed-loop payments network in the U.S. covers over 90% of fuel charging locations.
  • Sales Units: The Mobility segment processed 546.1 million payment transactions and 14,289.5 million gallons of fuel in 2025, reflecting a decrease from the previous year due to lower domestic fuel prices and macroeconomic factors.
  • New Product Launches: WEX expanded its product offerings with the 10-4 by WEX™ mobile app, providing nationwide diesel discounts and a truck-safe navigation tool. Additionally, the company advanced its Embedded Payments strategy beyond the travel vertical with new flexible funding capabilities.
  • New Production Launches: In November 2023, WEX introduced a cloud-native software solution for Mobility field service customers in industries like HVAC and roofing, enhancing capabilities such as scheduling, dispatch navigation, and payment acceptance.
  • Future Outlook: WEX aims to continue leveraging its technology and global footprint to simplify business operations and manage complexity. The company plans to focus on amplifying its core strengths, expanding its reach into adjacent workflows, and accelerating innovation to drive productivity and customer value.

Strategic Initiatives

  • WEX Inc. has undertaken several strategic initiatives to enhance its financial position and operational capabilities. In 2025, the company completed a private offering of $550 million in senior unsecured notes due in 2033 and amended its Credit Agreement to establish an incremental tranche of senior secured term loans amounting to $450 million. These funds were primarily used to fund a Tender Offer, repay $250 million outstanding under the Revolving Credit Facility, and cover related fees and expenses.
  • Additionally, WEX Inc. has focused on expanding its capabilities through acquisitions, such as the purchase of a factoring portfolio in January 2025, and plans to close on the acquisition of a card program portfolio in early 2026.
  • WEX Inc. has actively managed its capital through various initiatives. The company repurchased $801.6 million of its common stock in 2025, funded by borrowings from a new Term Loan B-3 facility and proceeds from the Senior Notes offering. The company also maintained a robust liquidity position with cash and cash equivalents of $905.8 million as of December 31, 2025, and had remaining borrowing availability of $1.1 billion under the Revolving Credit Facility. Additionally, WEX Bank's regulatory status allows it to raise capital by issuing deposits, with $5.4 billion in deposits as of the end of 2025.
  • Looking ahead, WEX Inc. plans to continue its focus on strategic acquisitions and capital investments to drive growth. The company expects to close on the acquisition of a card program portfolio in the first quarter of 2026, with an anticipated purchase price not exceeding $35 million. WEX Inc. also aims to maintain its liquidity and capital resources to support its operations and strategic initiatives, with a belief that its current financial condition and access to funding sources will be adequate to meet its cash needs for the foreseeable future. The company is committed to further enhancing its compliance management program as required by the FDIC's 2023 Order.

Challenges and Risks

  • The company faces significant risks related to fluctuations in fuel prices and demand, which are influenced by factors beyond its control, such as geopolitical conditions, economic downturns, and technological advancements in alternative fuels. The volatility in fuel prices directly impacts the company's revenue, particularly in its Mobility segment, which is heavily reliant on gasoline and diesel fuel transactions.
  • The company is also exposed to credit and fraud risks, especially in its Mobility segment, where it funds a counterparty's entire receivable or payable. The failure to adequately assess and monitor these risks could lead to substantial losses.
  • Regulatory risks are prominent, with the company subject to extensive supervision and regulation, particularly through WEX Bank. Changes in regulatory requirements or the loss of WEX Bank's industrial loan company charter could disrupt operations and increase costs.
  • Operational risks include the company's ability to adapt to technological changes and industry trends, such as the integration of electric vehicles (EVs) and artificial intelligence (AI). Failure to innovate or effectively implement new technologies could adversely affect the company's competitive position.
  • Management has identified the need to adapt to evolving market conditions and regulatory landscapes as a critical challenge. The company is investing in technology and risk management capabilities to mitigate these risks. However, there is no guarantee that these investments will yield the desired outcomes, and the company may face increased costs or operational disruptions.
  • The company is subject to market risks, including foreign currency exchange rate fluctuations, which could affect its financial results. The company's operations in multiple countries expose it to currency volatility, impacting revenues and expenses when translated into U.S. dollars.
  • Overall, the company must navigate a complex landscape of market, operational, and regulatory risks, with management actively seeking to address these challenges through strategic investments and risk management initiatives.

SEC Filing: WEX Inc. [ WEX ] - 10-K - Feb. 13, 2026

data.title: Polaris Inc. SEC 10-K Report
data.description:
Polaris Inc., a leading manufacturer of off-road vehicles, snowmobiles, motorcycles, and boats, has released its 2025 Form 10-K report. The report provides a comprehensive overview of the company's financial performance, business operations, strategic initiatives, and the challenges it faces in a competitive market environment.

Financial Highlights

Sales: Polaris Inc. reported sales of $7,152.0 million for 2025, remaining approximately flat compared to 2024. This stability was due to decreased shipments and lower net pricing driven by higher promotional costs, partially offset by a favorable product mix.

Gross Profit: The company recorded a gross profit of $1,368.7 million, a 7% decrease from $1,466.8 million in 2024. This decline was primarily due to incremental tariff charges, lower net pricing driven by higher promotional costs, and increased incentive compensation costs, partially offset by favorable operational costs and reduced warranty expense.

Operating (Loss) Income: Polaris Inc. reported an operating loss of $(348.7) million, compared to an operating income of $290.6 million in 2024. The loss was mainly due to impairment and other charges related to the Indian Motorcycle business being classified as held for sale, goodwill and other intangible asset impairment charges, and increased operating expenses.

Net (Loss) Income: The company reported a net loss of $(464.8) million, compared to a net income of $111.2 million in 2024. This significant decline was primarily due to impairment and other charges, incremental tariff charges, and increased incentive compensation costs.

Diluted Net (Loss) Income Per Share: Polaris Inc. reported a diluted net loss per share of $(8.18), compared to $1.95 in 2024, reflecting the net loss attributable to Polaris Inc. shareholders.

Business Highlights

Revenue Segments: Polaris Inc. operates in three business segments: Off Road, On Road, and Marine. The Off Road segment includes ORVs and snowmobiles, the On Road segment includes moto-roadsters and light-duty vehicles, and the Marine segment focuses on pontoon and deck boats.

Geographical Performance: Sales in the United States increased due to higher Marine and ORV shipments, while sales in Canada decreased primarily due to reduced snowmobile shipments. Sales in other countries decreased mainly due to reduced On Road shipments.

Sales Units: In 2025, North America ORV retail sales were estimated at 780,000 units, while worldwide ORV retail sales were approximately 970,000 units. Snowmobile sales for the 2025-2026 season-to-date period through December 31, 2025, increased significantly in North America.

New Product Launches: Key 2025 ORV product introductions included the RANGER 500 mid-size utility vehicle and updates within the RANGER XD 1500. The RZR portfolio saw a redesigned RZR XP and a new 72-inch wide RZR XP S. In the Marine segment, new models for Bennington, Godfrey, and Hurricane brands were launched.

Future Outlook: Polaris Inc. anticipates continued challenges due to tariffs and economic pressures but is focused on mitigating these impacts through sourcing alternatives and strategic pricing. The company remains committed to innovation and new product development to maintain its market position.

Strategic Initiatives

Strategic Divestiture: In 2025, Polaris Inc. entered into a definitive agreement to sell a majority interest in the Indian Motorcycle business, which was classified as held for sale. The sale closed in the first quarter of 2026. This strategic divestiture is part of the company's efforts to streamline operations and focus on core business segments.

Capital Management: Polaris Inc. did not repurchase shares of its common stock in open-market transactions during 2025, leaving $1.1 billion available for repurchase under the existing program. The company declared a quarterly cash dividend of $0.68 per share for the first quarter of 2026, marking a one percent increase from the prior quarterly dividend. In terms of debt management, the company prepaid all outstanding unsecured senior notes in June 2025 using proceeds from revolving loans under its unsecured credit facility. The company also issued $500 million aggregate principal amount of 5.60% Senior Notes due 2031 in November 2025.

Future Outlook: Polaris Inc. plans to continue its focus on improving liquidity and capital management by leveraging its credit facility and exploring long-term debt issuances. The company anticipates maintaining its strategy of increasing shareholder value through consistent dividend growth and potential share repurchases, subject to market conditions and financial performance. Additionally, the company is committed to evaluating the impact of tariffs and adjusting its supply chain strategies accordingly to ensure sustainable growth.

Challenges and Risks

Competitive Market: Polaris Inc. operates in highly competitive markets, facing intense competition in all product lines, which could negatively impact its business and operating results. The company relies on a network of approximately 2,400 independent dealers in North America and over 1,500 international dealers, which presents a risk if these relationships deteriorate due to weak economic conditions or reputational harm.

Macroeconomic Risks: Polaris faces macroeconomic risks, including sensitivity to economic conditions that impact customer spending, particularly in North America and Europe. Fluctuations in economic conditions, such as employment levels, inflation, and interest rates, could reduce spending on the company's products.

Operational Risks: The company is exposed to risks from shortages or increases in the cost of raw materials, commodities, and transportation, which could negatively impact its business. Trade matters, including tariffs, pose additional risks, as they may increase the cost of goods and reduce the company's ability to sell products globally. The company also faces operational risks, such as disruptions in suppliers' operations, which could disrupt production schedules.

Regulatory Risks: Regulatory risks include compliance with extensive safety, environmental, and trade regulations, which could harm the company's reputation and financial condition if not adhered to.

Market Risks: Polaris is exposed to fluctuations in foreign currency exchange rates, which could result in declines in reported sales and net earnings. The company actively manages this exposure by entering into foreign exchange hedging contracts. Retail credit market deterioration and volatility may restrict the ability of retail customers to finance the purchase of Polaris products, potentially impacting sales and profitability. The company also faces risks related to its outstanding debt and restrictive covenants in its debt agreements, which could limit operating flexibility and increase vulnerability to interest rate increases.

SEC Filing: Polaris Inc. [ PII ] - 10-K - Feb. 13, 2026

data.title: CARLISLE COMPANIES INC SEC 10-K Report
data.description:
Carlisle Companies Inc., a leading manufacturer of premium building products, has released its 2025 Form 10-K report, detailing the company's financial performance, business operations, strategic initiatives, and the challenges it faces. The report provides a comprehensive overview of Carlisle's efforts to drive growth and innovation in the non-residential construction market, despite a complex economic environment.

Financial Highlights

Revenues: $5,019.9 million, reflecting a slight increase from the previous year, primarily due to higher sales in the non-residential construction end-market.

Gross Profit: $1,792.6 million, with a gross margin of 35.7%, down from 37.7% in 2024, primarily due to increased unit costs resulting from higher absorption of fixed costs on lower volumes.

Operating Income: $1,002.5 million, with an operating margin of 20.0%, a decrease from 22.8% in 2024, impacted by higher operating costs and increased research and development expenses.

Net Income: $740.7 million, a decrease from $1,311.8 million in 2024, influenced by lower income from continuing operations and a decrease in interest income.

Diluted Earnings Per Share from Continuing Operations: $17.16, compared to $18.34 in 2024, reflecting the overall decrease in net income.

Business Highlights

Revenue Segments: Carlisle Construction Materials (CCM) and Carlisle Weatherproofing Technologies (CWT) are the two main segments. CCM focuses on premium roofing products and related technologies, while CWT provides high-performance waterproofing and moisture protection products.

Geographical Performance: CCM operates primarily in the United States, with additional facilities in Germany, the Netherlands, the U.K., and Romania. CWT's primary markets are the United States and Canada.

Sales Units: CCM's sales are driven by the need to replace older roofs on non-residential structures, with re-roofing demand representing approximately 70% of its commercial roofing business.

New Product Launches: The company emphasizes innovation, particularly in energy-efficient products, systems, and solutions for sustainable buildings, aligning with the Vision 2030 strategy.

Future Outlook: Despite challenges in new construction and a complex economic environment, Carlisle remains confident in achieving its Vision 2030 financial objectives, focusing on innovation, operational excellence, and capital allocation.

Operational Strategy: The Carlisle Operating System (COS) is a key component, focusing on lean enterprise and six sigma principles to drive efficiencies and operating leverage across the company.

Sustainability Initiatives: Carlisle is committed to sustainability, aiming to produce energy-efficient products, reduce operational emissions, and divert construction waste from landfills, with a net-zero GHG emission goal.

Human Capital Resources: The company employs approximately 5,900 people, with a focus on attracting, developing, and retaining talent through partnerships with educational institutions and a commitment to diversity and safety.

Strategic Initiatives

Strategic Initiatives: Carlisle Companies Incorporated continued to execute its Vision 2030 strategy, focusing on innovation, operational excellence, and strategic acquisitions. The company increased investments in innovation to develop new market-leading products and expanded automation in factories to drive operational excellence. Recent acquisitions, such as Bonded Logic and ThermaFoam, align with the strategic pivot to a pure play building products company, enhancing Carlisle's product offerings and geographic reach.

Capital Management: In 2025, Carlisle generated over $1 billion in operating cash flow, which was used to repurchase $1.3 billion of its own shares, reflecting a strong commitment to returning value to shareholders. The company also issued $987.8 million in long-term debt through the issuance of 5.25% notes due 2035 and 5.55% notes due 2040, while redeeming $400 million of 3.50% notes. Dividends paid amounted to $181.1 million, demonstrating a continued focus on shareholder returns.

Future Outlook: Carlisle anticipates challenging market conditions to persist into the first half of 2026 but remains confident in its ability to generate strong returns and pursue value-enhancing acquisitions. The company plans to maintain its balanced capital deployment approach, focusing on investments in its businesses, strategic acquisitions, and continued dividend increases. Carlisle's leadership position in the North American building-products market and its robust cash flow are expected to support these initiatives.

Challenges and Risks

Challenges and Risks: Carlisle Companies Incorporated faces significant challenges in maintaining its growth strategy, which heavily relies on synergistic acquisitions. The competitive nature of acquisitions often results in high valuations, and there is no assurance of identifying, negotiating, and financing suitable acquisitions at reasonable values. Additionally, the company is highly concentrated in the construction market, which is sensitive to economic conditions, interest rates, and government spending. This concentration poses a risk if there is a downturn in construction activities.

The company is exposed to several strategic, business, and operational risks. A key risk is the dependency on a few large customers in niche markets, which could lead to adverse effects if there is a loss or significant decline in business with these customers. The company also faces risks from potential disruptions to its manufacturing facilities due to natural disasters, regulatory issues, or cybersecurity breaches. Furthermore, the development and introduction of new products are critical to the company's Vision 2030 strategic plan, but these efforts may encounter difficulties and delays, impacting financial performance.

Industry and macroeconomic risks include the cyclical nature of the markets served, which are sensitive to economic conditions. The company is also vulnerable to fluctuations in raw material costs, particularly petroleum-based products, which could impact profitability if costs cannot be passed on to customers. Environmental, regulatory, and legal risks are present due to stringent environmental laws and potential liabilities from past industrial activities. Global climate change regulations could increase compliance costs and impact the company's reputation if commitments to reduce GHG emissions are not met.

Despite headwinds in new construction and a complex economic environment, Carlisle continues to execute its Vision 2030 strategy. The company is focused on innovation, operational excellence through the Carlisle Operating System, and capital allocation. However, the challenging M&A environment in 2025 led to a focus on share repurchases. The company remains well-positioned in the North American market, benefiting from strong re-roofing demand and the need for energy-efficient solutions. Management anticipates continued challenging market conditions into the first half of 2026 but remains confident in generating strong returns and pursuing value-enhancing acquisitions.

Carlisle is exposed to market risks, including changes in interest rates, foreign currency exchange rates, and commodity prices. The company manages interest rate risk through fixed-rate debt and has no current interest rate swaps. Foreign currency exchange risk is managed on a consolidated basis, with potential hedging through forward contracts. Commodity price risk is addressed by seeking price increases, altering raw material mixes, or entering commodity-linked contracts. The company currently has no derivative financial instruments in place to mitigate these risks.

SEC Filing: CARLISLE COMPANIES INC [ CSL ] - 10-K - Feb. 13, 2026

data.title: C. H. ROBINSON WORLDWIDE, INC. SEC 10-K Report
data.description:
C.H. Robinson Worldwide, Inc., a global leader in third-party logistics and supply chain management, has released its 2025 10-K report, detailing the company's financial performance, business operations, strategic initiatives, and the challenges it faces in the competitive transportation services industry. The report provides a comprehensive overview of the company's efforts to optimize its operations, enhance profitability, and navigate market dynamics.

Financial Highlights

Total Revenues: $16,232.8 million, reflecting an 8.4% decrease from the previous year primarily due to the divestiture of the Europe Surface Transportation business and lower pricing and volume in ocean services.

Gross Profits: $2,671.2 million, a decrease of 1.8% from the prior year, driven by lower adjusted gross profit per transaction in ocean services and the divestiture of the Europe Surface Transportation business.

Operating Income: $794.961 million, an increase of 18.8% from the previous year, attributed to a decrease in operating expenses.

Net Income: $587.081 million, up 26.1% from the previous year, benefiting from higher foreign tax credits and cost optimization efforts.

Diluted Net Income Per Share: $4.83, representing a 25.1% increase, supported by improved operating margins and reduced interest expenses.

Business Highlights

Revenue Segments: C.H. Robinson operates through two main segments: North American Surface Transportation (NAST) and Global Forwarding. NAST provides transportation and logistics services across North America, while Global Forwarding offers international logistics services, including ocean and air freight.

Geographical Performance: The company operates globally, with significant operations in North America, Europe, Asia, Oceania, South America, and the Middle East. The divestiture of the Europe Surface Transportation business in early 2025 impacted revenue and operational metrics.

Sales Units: In 2025, C.H. Robinson managed approximately 37 million shipments for 75,000 customers, utilizing over 450,000 contract carriers on its platform.

New Product Launches: In November 2024, C.H. Robinson launched Managed Solutions to provide seamless access to 4PL services, 3PL managed transportation, and transportation management system technology.

Operational Efficiency: The company has enhanced its dynamic pricing and costing models, allowing for more rapid response to market fluctuations and improved adjusted gross profit per transaction in LTL, truckload, and customs services.

Future Outlook: The company anticipates continued pressure on ocean pricing due to excess vessel capacity and weak global demand. However, advancements in dynamic pricing and costing capabilities are expected to support operational performance.

Human Capital: As of December 31, 2025, C.H. Robinson employed 11,855 people across 37 countries, with a focus on leveraging Lean AI to enhance operational efficiency and employee productivity.

Environmental Sustainability: C.H. Robinson is committed to reducing greenhouse gas emissions and has exceeded its goal to reduce Scope 1 and 2 carbon intensity by 40% by 2025, achieving this two years early.

Strategic Initiatives

Strategic Initiatives: C.H. Robinson Worldwide, Inc. has focused on optimizing its capital structure by divesting its Europe Surface Transportation business, which was completed on February 1, 2025. This strategic move was aimed at streamlining operations and focusing on core business areas. The company has also been advancing its dynamic pricing and costing capabilities to better respond to market fluctuations, which has improved adjusted gross profit per transaction in its transportation services.

Capital Management: The company has been actively managing its capital through a combination of debt reduction and shareholder returns. In 2025, C.H. Robinson reduced its outstanding borrowings by $289 million, primarily by decreasing the balance on its Receivables Securitization Facility and Revolving Credit Facility. The company also resumed share repurchases, buying back $354.7 million of its own stock, and increased its annual dividend to shareholders. Additionally, the company maintains a $1 billion revolving credit facility, which remains undrawn as of December 31, 2025, providing significant liquidity. The company has also been managing its interest expenses effectively, with a decrease in interest expense due to lower average debt balances and variable interest rates.

Future Outlook: Looking ahead, C.H. Robinson plans to continue its focus on capital efficiency and shareholder value. The company anticipates capital expenditures in 2026 to be approximately $75 million to $85 million, primarily for investments in software and technology to enhance operational capabilities. The Board of Directors has approved an additional $2 billion authorization under the company's share repurchase program, which is expected to be executed over the next three years. The company remains committed to its quarterly dividend and share repurchase strategy to enhance shareholder value, while also exploring opportunities for strategic acquisitions that align with its growth objectives.

Challenges and Risks

Challenges and Risks: C.H. Robinson Worldwide, Inc. operates in a highly competitive and fragmented transportation services industry, facing both traditional and non-traditional competitors. The company relies heavily on technology, including AI and machine learning, to maintain its competitive edge. However, failure to keep pace with technological advancements could result in a competitive disadvantage. The company also depends on third-party transportation providers, which exposes it to risks related to service quality and availability.

Economic Recession: Economic recession poses a significant risk, potentially reducing freight volumes and impacting growth opportunities. The company faces credit risks from customers who may experience economic difficulties, leading to increased working capital needs. Transportation provider failures could disrupt service commitments. Fluctuating fuel costs and higher carrier prices may decrease profit margins. The company is also vulnerable to cybersecurity threats, which could lead to service interruptions and reputational damage. International operations expose the company to geopolitical risks, regulatory changes, and foreign currency fluctuations. Climate change presents physical, compliance, and reputational risks, potentially affecting operations and customer demands.

Management's Discussion and Analysis: Management highlights the importance of adapting to market conditions and technological changes to maintain operational efficiency. The company is focused on leveraging AI to enhance service delivery and operational processes. However, the rapid pace of technological change and the need for skilled personnel in AI and cybersecurity remain challenges. The company is also addressing risks related to climate change by aligning its sustainability efforts with stakeholder needs and regulatory requirements.

Market Risks: C.H. Robinson is exposed to market risks, including interest rate fluctuations and foreign currency exchange rate changes, which could adversely affect financial results. The company is also subject to regulatory risks, with potential changes in tax laws and environmental regulations impacting operations and profitability.

SEC Filing: C. H. ROBINSON WORLDWIDE, INC. [ CHRW ] - 10-K - Feb. 13, 2026

data.title: ROKU Stock Is Rallying Today – What Is Making Traders So Bullish?
data.description:
Key points:
  • JPMorgan analyst Cory Carpenter reiterated Roku as a top pick with an ‘Overweight’ rating.
  • Rosenblatt double upgraded Roku to ‘Buy’ from ‘Neutral’ with a price target of $118, up from $106.
  • Wedbush noted that Roku is on its Best Ideas List given accelerating monetization with an underappreciated runway.

ROKU stock rallied on Friday after it got price hikes from nine analysts and a double upgrade from another post its strong earnings. 

At the time of writing, stock rallied 9%. 

The company on Friday said it expects platform revenue in Q1 2026 to grow more than 21% year-over-year and devices segment revenue down mid-single digits to drive its total net revenue of $1.200 billion, up roughly 18% from a year ago.

Buoyant Analysts

Rosenblatt double upgraded Roku to ‘Buy’ from ‘Neutral’ with a price target of $118, up from $106. Roku "handily beat" Q4 estimates and guided above expectations for Q1 and 2026, said the analyst, who added that the outlook "looks beatable again." The current valuation is "too cheap" for a company beating and raising earnings before interest, taxes, depreciation, and amortization (EBITDA) currently projected to grow at a 46% compound annual growth rate from 2025-2027, the analyst contended. 

UBS raised the firm's price target on Roku to $110 from $103 and kept a ‘Neutral’ rating on the shares.

Wedbush also was among the firms who raised the firm's price target on Roku to $140 from $130 and kept an ‘Outperform’ rating on the shares. The firm noted that Roku is on Wedbush's Best Ideas List given accelerating monetization with an underappreciated runway. 

JPMorgan analyst Cory Carpenter reiterated Roku as a top pick with an ‘Overweight’ rating and $125 price target post the Q4 report. 

Q4 Report

Roku reported a 16% jump in its fourth-quarter revenue on a year-over-year basis. The reported total revenue came at $1.39 billion while analysts had expected a revenue of $1.35 billion, as per data from fiscal.ai. 

The company also swung to profit of $0.53 per diluted share, compared to a loss of $0.24 per share from the year-ago period. Analysts expected a profit of $0.28 per share. 

How Did Stocktwits Users React?

Retail sentiment around ROKU trended in ‘extremely bullish’ territory amid ‘extremely high’ message volume. 

One user predicted the stock to cross the $100 mark next week.

https://stocktwits.com/TheInimitableInvestor/message/644953748

Another user said that the stock has been consolidating over the past four years and they predicted the stock to cross the $200 mark. 

https://stocktwits.com/cccxInvestor/message/644950665

Shares in the company have risen 10.6% over the past year.

data.title: PBB Shares Plunge 10.8% After 30--40M 2026 Profit Outlook
data.description:
Deutsche Pfandbriefbank AG (PBBGF) shares came under heavy pressure after the lender issued a markedly softer profit outlook for 2026, a signal that its restructuring path could take longer than some investors had anticipated. The bank said it expects full-year profit before taxes of between 30 million and 40 million, well below analyst forecasts of more than 100 million. The reaction was swift: the stock fell as much as 10.8% to an all-time intraday low and was still down 9.4% by 2:58 p.m. in Frankfurt, underscoring how sharply expectations may have reset.

Management attributed the muted guidance in part to the high costs associated with a significant risk transfer tied to its efforts to reduce US commercial real estate exposure. In December, PBB accessed the SRT market, with Brookfield-owned Oaktree Capital Management writing protection on a $320 million mezzanine tranche linked to a $2 billion loan portfolio. The deal was priced at a spread of more than 15 percentage points over a borrowing benchmark, according to people familiar with the matter at the time, compared with spreads of less than 10 percentage points for the majority of SRT transactions in the prior 12 months, based on data from Seer Capital Management LP. The bank also cited a sluggish recovery in real estate markets, noting that income lost from its US business will likely only be gradually compensated by new activity in Europe.

The outlook follows a reported pretax loss of 250 million for 2025, which was within the company's forecast range. PBB said it does not see scope for a dividend payment for last year, though it added that conditions for servicing its AT1 instrument are currently in place, with a decision due in April. Chief Executive Officer Kay Wolf is overseeing a strategic overhaul that includes exiting the US roughly a decade after expanding there and refocusing on European markets while expanding into asset management. That earlier US push resulted in a large portfolio of commercial real estate loans, a strategy that later faced pressure as the pandemic reduced office demand and higher interest rates increased financing costs, factors that could continue to shape the bank's earnings trajectory as it attempts to rebuild profitability.

data.title: Moderna Challenges FDA After Flu Shot Review Refusal, 26% Covid Drop
data.description:
Moderna is escalating its standoff with US regulators at a delicate juncture in its post-pandemic reset. On Friday's analyst call, Chief Executive Officer Stephane Bancel described the Food and Drug Administration as increasingly unpredictable, cautioning that the current regulatory posture could threaten US leadership in innovative medicines. The criticism followed the agency's decision not to review Moderna's flu vaccine application based on its mRNA platform, a product management has positioned as central to broadening the company beyond its shrinking Covid franchise. Chief Financial Officer James Mock acknowledged that with the flu shot's path now uncertain, it is too early to determine whether Moderna can still reach its stated goal of breaking even by 2028.

The FDA said it declined to review the filing because Moderna's studies did not compare the flu shot to the best-available standard of care. FDA Commissioner Marty Makary stated in a television interview that the outcome should not have been unexpected, arguing the company did not follow the agency's recommendations. Moderna, however, has countered that regulators had previously approved the study design and that expectations have since shifted. The flu shot remains under review in Europe, Canada and Australia, but in the US the decision adds a layer of uncertainty at a time when mRNA technology has become a political flashpoint during President Donald Trump's second term.

Against that backdrop, the latest financials suggest some operational stabilization, even as vaccine demand normalizes. Moderna reported a fourth-quarter adjusted loss of $2.11 per share, narrower than a year earlier, on revenue of $678 million, exceeding the $625 million average Wall Street estimate as US Covid uptake fell less than expected. Mock previously said the number of Americans receiving Covid shots dropped 26% in 2025, a smaller decline than the company had projected. Research and development expenses declined 31% in the quarter as late-stage respiratory trials wound down, reflecting ongoing cost controls. With its RSV vaccine struggling to gain traction and eligibility for Covid vaccines narrowing in the US, investors are now focused on whether Moderna can navigate the current turbulence and advance its oncology collaboration with Merck & Co., where late-stage melanoma data could arrive later this year.

data.title: Indexes Head for Weekly Losses; Inflation Slows to 2.4% — WSJ
data.description:
By Caitlin McCabe

Stock indexes rose Friday, stabilizing after the previous session's AI-driven selloff. All three benchmarks were on course for weekly losses, with the Nasdaq down the most.

New data showed inflation slowed a touch more than expected last month, though not if volatile food and energy prices are excluded. Some economists had braced for hotter readings, as prices have a habit of spiking at the start of the year.

The report showed consumer-price growth slowed to an annual pace of 2.4% in January, undershooting forecasts for 2.5%, as prices for gas dropped, alongside costs for used cars and trucks. The core rate was 2.5%, matching forecasts.

Ten-year Treasury yields slipped, hitting their lowest intraday levels since early December.

In global markets, gold and silver prices rose. Asian stocks sold off, tracking yesterday's U.S. moves, while European indexes were mixed. Bitcoin climbed about 5% to about $68,900.

This item is part of a Wall Street Journal live coverage event. The full stream can be found by searching P/WSJL (WSJ Live Coverage).

data.title: Electrovaya Shares Fall After 1Q Revenue Miss
data.description:
By Elias Schisgall

Shares of Electrovaya fell after the company's fiscal first-quarter revenue fell short of Wall Street estimates.

Shares were down 10% in Toronto to 10.68 Canadian dollars in Friday afternoon trading. The stock has more than tripled in value over the past year.

The Canadian lithium-ion battery company posted a fiscal first-quarter profit of US$1 million, or 2 cents a share, compared with a loss of about $400,000, or 1 cent a share, a year earlier. Analysts polled by FactSet were expecting earnings of 1 cent a share.

Revenue grew 39% to $15.5 million, up from $11.1 million a year earlier but short of analysts' expectations of $16.7 million. The company said the first quarter of its fiscal year tends to generate lower revenue due to seasonality.

For the full fiscal year, the company is expecting revenue to grow more than 30% to more than $83 million, citing sustained battery demand and expansion into new markets.

Write to Elias Schisgall at elias.schisgall@wsj.com

data.title: Wheat, soybean futures ease after rally; corn steady-higher
data.description:
Key points:
  • Wheat slips after two-day short-covering bounce
  • Reminders of ample global wheat supplies add pressure
  • Soybeans pause rally; US-China trade relations remain in focus
  • US holiday weekend and Chinese New Year set to slow trade

U.S. wheat and soybean futures fell on Friday, retreating from multi-month highs set a day earlier as traders booked profits and squared positions ahead of a long U.S. holiday weekend and the upcoming Lunar New Year holidays in China, analysts said.

Reminders of ample global wheat supplies and news that India would allow wheat exports added pressure. Corn futures edged higher.

As of 12:58 p.m. CST (1858 GMT), Chicago Board of Trade March wheat futures (WH26) were down 3 cents at $5.49-1/2 per bushel, after peaking on Thursday at $5.53-1/2, the contract's highest since November 20.

CBOT March soybeans (SH26) were down 2 cents at $11.35-1/4 a bushel while March corn (CH26) was up 3/4 cent at $4.32 a bushel.

Wheat declined after a sharp two-session climb that analysts attributed to short-covering. Open interest in CBOT wheat futures fell on Wednesday and Thursday as prices rose, CBOT data showed, a sign of traders liquidating short positions. Trading volume was heavy on the two-day rally, with more than 300,000 CBOT wheat contracts traded each day.

On Friday, outlooks for large global wheat harvests in 2026 weighed on prices. IKAR, a consultancy, raised its forecast of the 2026 wheat crop in Russia, the world's biggest exporter, to 91 million metric tons, from 88 million previously.

Ratings for winter crops in France, the European Union's biggest grower, hit their best in three years, farm office FranceAgriMer data showed.

India's government allowed the export of 2.5 million metric tons of wheat as the world's second-biggest producer sought to support local growers amid protests over a trade deal between New Delhi and Washington.

Following this week's run of short-covering, "these bearish global supply fundamentals are going to come back to the forefront," said Randy Place, an analyst at Hightower Report.

Soybeans ticked lower on pre-weekend profit-taking a day after the CBOT March soybean contract (SH26) reached its highest since early December. The arrival of a bumper Brazilian harvest anchored prices as well.

Soybeans rallied after U.S. President Donald Trump said last week that China had increased its target for U.S. soybean purchases, and a report in the South China Morning Post on Thursday said Trump and Chinese President Xi Jinping could extend their countries' trade truce for as long as a year.

Corn futures clung to modest gains, supported by a strong U.S. export pace and concerns about grain quality in China, Place said.

Chinese buyers have ramped up feed grain purchases in recent months, taking large volumes of Australian barley and U.S. sorghum after rain damaged the domestic corn harvest, trade sources said.

data.title: Amazon Slides 18%, Erases $470 Billion Amid $200 Billion Spending Plan
data.description:
Amazon.com Inc. is now pressing up against a threshold it has not touched in nearly two decades. The shares fell another 0.8% on Friday, putting them on track for a ninth consecutive decline. If the session finishes in negative territory, it would mark the company's longest daily losing streak since a nine-day slide that ended in July 2006. Over this stretch, the stock has dropped more than 18%, erasing over $470 billion in market value and pushing shares to their lowest level since May.

The drawdown appears closely linked to results released earlier this month, when Amazon outlined plans to spend $200 billion this year on data centers, chips and other equipment a level that came in well above expectations. That capital intensity has sharpened the market's focus on cash flow durability. Anthony Saglimbene, chief market strategist at Ameriprise, cautioned that if Amazon's spending leads to negative cash flow, it could represent a major concern and a red flag, and investors are increasingly viewing it through that lens.

The scrutiny extends beyond a single name. Investors have grown more sensitive to artificial intelligence-related capital spending across large-cap technology, a theme that has also weighed on Microsoft and Alphabet. The four biggest spenders Amazon, Alphabet , Microsoft and Meta Platforms have together projected roughly $650 billion in capital expenditures in 2026. Saglimbene suggested that if these companies generate negative or weaker cash flow as a result of that investment cycle, it could alter how they are valued, particularly during periods of market stress or if AI evolves in ways the market does not anticipate. Amazon shares are down about 17% this month, potentially marking their steepest monthly decline since April 2022, versus a 3.2% drop in the Nasdaq 100 Index in February.

data.title: Stuve Gold Corp. Provides Update on Proposed Private Placement
data.description:
(TheNewswire)

CALGARY, ALBERTA – February 13,2026 – TheNewswire -Stuve Gold Corp. (“Stuve Gold” or the“Corporation”) (TSXV:STUV) announces that, further to its newsrelease dated February 11, 2026, with respect to its proposedprivate placement of up to 12,500,000 units(“Units”) for gross proceeds of up to $3,000,000 (the“Offering”), the Corporation has determined, pursuant tothe TSX Venture Exchange's "part andparcel" pricing rules, that the share purchase warrantscomprising part of the Units will entitle the holder to purchaseadditional Common Shares for a period of one (1) year from the date ofclosing of the Offering at a price of $0.38 per share.  For furtherinformation with respect to the terms of the Offering, please see theCorporation's news release dated February 11, 2026.

Proceeds from the Offering, assuming the maximumOffering, will be used to conduct exploration activities on the LasAnimas property ($1,000,000), Stuve Gold’s existing properties($1,500,000), as well as for expenses related to the Offering, for thepossible acquisition of other properties, for repayment of debtobligations, and for general working capital purposes (up to$500,000). If less than the maximum Offering is raised, proceeds fromthe Offering will be used first to conduct exploration activities onthe Las Animas property, then on the existing properties, and then forexpenses of the Offering, possible acquisitions, repayment of debtobligations and general working capital purposes.  

The Offering is being offered to all of the existingshareholders of Stuve Gold who are permitted to subscribe pursuant tothe Existing Shareholder Exemption. This offer is open until March 27,2026, or such other date or dates as the Corporation determines and one or more closings are expectedto occur, with the first closing anticipated for on or about February24, 2026.

Any existing shareholders interested in participatingin the Offering should contact the Corporation pursuant to the contactinformation set forth below.

The Corporation has set February 12, 2026, as therecord date for determining existing shareholders entitled tosubscribe for Units pursuant to the Existing Shareholder Exemption.Subscribers purchasing Units under the Existing Shareholder Exemptionwill need to represent in writing that they meet certain requirementsof the Existing Shareholder Exemption, including that they were, on orbefore the record date, a shareholder of the Corporation and still area shareholder as at theclosing date. The aggregate acquisition cost to a subscriber under theExisting Shareholder Exemption cannot exceed $15,000 unless thatsubscriber has obtained advice from a registered investment dealerregarding the suitability of the investment.

As the Corporation is also relying on the Exemption forSales to Purchasers Advised by Investment Dealers, it confirms thatthere is no material fact or material change related to theCorporation which has not been generally disclosed. In addition tooffering the Units pursuant to the Existing Shareholder Exemption andthe Exemption for Sales to Purchasers Advised by Investment Dealers,the Units are also being offered pursuant to other availableprospectus exemptions, including sales to accredited investors. Unlessthe Corporation determines to increase the gross proceeds of theOffering, if subscriptions received for the Offering based onall available exemptions exceed the maximumOffering amount of $3,000,000, Units will be allocated pro rata among allsubscribers qualifying under all available exemptions.

The Common Shares, Warrants and Broker Warrants issuedpursuant to the Offering will be subject to a four month and one dayhold period. Completion of the Offering remains subject to theapproval of the TSX Venture Exchange.

About Stuve Gold

Stuve Gold is advancing mineral properties in Chilethat hold promising potential for gold, copper, silver, cobalt anduranium accumulations exhibited by historical mining activities on, orassociated with, those properties.  

Stuve Gold's Common Shares are listed on the TSX-Vunder the symbol "STUV". More information on Stuve Gold maybe viewed on www.sedarplus.ca or theCorporation's website www.stuvegoldcorp.ca.

Cautionary Note RegardingForward-Looking Statements

This news release includes certain"forward-looking statements" under applicable Canadiansecurities legislation. Forward-looking statements include, but arenot limited to, the Corporation's ability to obtain necessaryapprovals from the TSX Venture Exchange with respect to the Offering,the successful completion of the Offering and the use of proceeds ofthe Offering.   Forward-looking statements are necessarily based upona number of estimates and assumptions that, while consideredreasonable, are subject to known and unknown risks, uncertainties andother factors which may cause the actual results and future events todiffer materially from those expressed or implied by suchforward-looking statements. Such factors include, but are not limitedto: general business, economic, competitive, political and socialuncertainties; delay or failure to receive board, shareholder orregulatory approvals; the price of metals including gold, silvercopper, cobalt and uranium; and the results of exploration programs.There can be no assurance that such statements will prove to beaccurate, as actual results and future events could differ materiallyfrom those anticipated in such statements. Accordingly, readers shouldnot place undue reliance on forward-looking statements. Stuve Golddisclaims any intention or obligation to update or revise anyforward-looking statements, whether as a result of new information,future events or otherwise, except as required by law.

Neither the TSXVenture Exchange nor its Regulation Services Provider (as that term isdefined in the policies of the TSX Venture Exchange) acceptsresponsibility for the adequacy or accuracy of this release.

data.title: SpaceX considering dual-class shares in IPO, Bloomberg News reports
data.description:
Elon Musk's SpaceX is considering a dual-class share structure in its planned IPO this year, Bloomberg News reported on Friday, citing people familiar with the matter.

The space firm, which acquired Musk's artificial-intelligence startup xAI, is planning a blockbuster public offering this year that could value it at over $1.5 trillion.

Companies with dual-class structures have two or more types of shares with different voting rights — usually one with greater voting rights for founders or early investors and another for other shareholders with less voting power.

SpaceX is also in the process of adding members to its board of directors, the Bloomberg report said, to oversee the IPO process and expand Musk's space goals outside its main rocket and satellite business.

Deliberations are ongoing and details of the IPO could change, the report added.

SpaceX did not immediately respond to a Reuters request for comment.

Though EV company Tesla does not currently have a dual-class voting structure, Musk has said he needs to own a significant stake - roughly 25% - to have enough voting influence.

data.title: Urals diffs stable, CPC Blend exports set to rise in March
data.description:
Urals crude differentials were unchanged on Friday, while CPC Blend oil exports were set to rise in March, traders said.

The Caspian Pipeline Consortium (CPC) plans to increase CPC Blend crude exports in March to about 1.7 million barrels per day (bpd) from 1.1 million bpd in the revised February plan, three industry sources told Reuters.

CPC oil terminal loadings returned to full capacity in the end of January, but the grade's exports remained low amid output issues at Tengiz oil field.

Oil production at Tengiz, the main source for CPC Blend oil stream, is gradually recovering and is expected to return to normal levels next month.

PLATTS WINDOW

  • No bids or offers were made on Friday for Urals, Azeri BTC and CPC Blend, traders said.

NEWS

  • Slovakia has seen an interruption of oil supplies via the Druzhba pipeline into eastern Europe but expects the renewal of flows in the coming days, the Slovak economy ministry said on Friday.

  • OPEC+ is leaning towards a resumption in oil output increases from April, three OPEC+ sources said, as the group prepares for peak summer demand and price strength is bolstered by tensions over U.S.-Iran relations.

data.title: Yanik Guillemette of Accolad Announces Major Integration to Automate Referral Payments and Commercial Incentives
data.description:
Montreal, Quebec--(Newsfile Corp. - February 13, 2026) - Yanik Guillemette, technology entrepreneur and Chair of Accolad's External Advisory Committee on Technology and Innovation, today announced the deployment of a new automation infrastructure enabling Canadian organizations to instantly manage referral payments, sales incentives, and customer rewards through an expanded digital gift card marketplace.

Yanik Guillemette Québec Entrepreneur

To view an enhanced version of this graphic, please visit:

https://images.newsfilecorp.com/files/11862/283842_44fe7acbc4267bc8_001full.jpg

This development marks an important milestone for Accolad, whose platform now provides access to more than 200 international digital gift cards while also promoting a strong local ecosystem featuring over 70 recognized Québec-based businesses.

This allows organizations to balance operational simplicity with support for local commerce.

Automating What Was Previously Manual

Historically, referral programs and commercial incentive payments relied on fragmented processes, including manual transfers, physical gift cards, heavy administrative tracking, and significant delays.

Accolad's new integration is designed to eliminate these operational frictions.

"Organizations want to reward people quickly - whether it's a referring customer, a salesperson exceeding targets, or a partner closing a transaction. Until now, these payments were surprisingly complex to manage. Our goal was to make them as simple as sending an email," said Yanik Guillemette.

The platform now enables:

  • Automated referral payments;
  • Sales contests and performance challenges management;
  • Distribution of digital commercial incentives;
  • Rapid issuance of compensations or promotional rewards;
  • Integration of incentives directly into automated customer journeys.

Growing Adoption Across Travel and Insurance Industries

Accolad is seeing accelerating adoption in sectors where financial incentives play a strategic role, including:

  • Tour operators and travel agencies;
  • Insurance providers;
  • Insurance brokers;
  • Partner and affiliate networks.

These organizations now use the platform to deliver digital bonuses and rewards to clients or partners, significantly simplifying administrative compliance and accounting processes.

"In many industries, issuing a bonus or reward remains unexpectedly complicated. Our objective is to transform these payments into an instant, traceable, and fully automated process," added Guillemette.

Supporting Local Economies in a Digital Environment

Beyond automation, Accolad promotes a hybrid approach combining global brands with local merchants.

According to the company, this strategy enables organizations to offer meaningful rewards while supporting Québec businesses - an increasingly important factor for employers and corporate partners.

Infrastructure Designed for the Relationship Economy

This integration reflects a broader trend: the rise of the relationship economy, where growth increasingly depends on referrals, partnerships, and customer loyalty alongside traditional acquisition strategies.

Accolad positions its technology as an invisible infrastructure layer enabling large-scale automation of incentive micro-payments.

"Referral programs and commercial incentives are becoming major growth drivers. Our role is to remove operational complexity so organizations can focus on building stronger relationships with customers and partners," concluded Yanik Guillemette.

About Accolad

Accolad is a Canadian technology platform specializing in the automation of recognition programs cards, digital rewards, and commercial incentives. The solution enables organizations to securely distribute rewards and digital payments at scale through an extensive catalogue of local and international brands.

Media Contact

Julie Tessier

Head of Communications

Accolad

Email: j.tessier@accolad.com

Phone: 579-274-1477

To view the source version of this press release, please visit https://www.newsfilecorp.com/release/283842

data.title: Swiss Market Ends Moderately Higher
data.description:
BRUSSELS (dpa-AFX) - Switzerland stock market's benchmark index SMI settled with a solid gain on Friday after staying positive right through the day's session. In addition to reacting to some corporate news, investors digested the nation's inflation data.

The index, which moved between 13,532.37 and 13,634.11, ended at 13,600.67, gaining 70.75 points or 0.52%.

Lonza Group climbed 4.7%, Schindler gained about 3.25%, Kuehne + Nagel and VAT Group ended higher by about 3%.

Logitech International and SGS ended higher by 2.67% and 2.28%, respectively.

Swisscom gained about 2.35%. The company reported fiscal 2025 net income of CHF1.27 billion, compared to CHF1.54 billion in the prior year, a decline of 17.6%. EBITDA after lease expense rose by 22.6% to 4.98 billion francs, while Revenue increased by 36.6% to 15.05 billion francs.

Geberit, Amrize, Galderma Group, Novartis, Givaudan and Partners Group also posted strong gains.

Sonova ended down by 1.49%. Straumann Holding closed 1.1% down, while, Richemont and Helvetia Baloise Holding ended lower by 0.87% and 0.84%, respectively.

Data from the Federal Statistical Office showed Switzerland's consumer price inflation held steady at the start of the year as expected, rising 0.1% on a yearly basis in January, the same as in the previous month.

Prices for housing and energy grew 0.8% from last year, and education costs were 2.6% more expensive. Meanwhile, prices for food and non-alcoholic beverages dropped 0.4%, and transport charges declined 2%.

On a monthly basis, consumer prices edged down 0.1% amid lower costs for electricity and accommodation. Economists were looking for flat change as in December.

Copyright(c) 2026 RTTNews.com. All Rights Reserved

Copyright RTT News/dpa-AFX

data.title: US judge orders Trump administration to facilitate deported college student's return
data.description:
By Nate Raymond

A U.S. federal judge on Friday ordered the Trump administration to facilitate the return of a Honduran college student who was deported in violation of a court order, a step the government had previously refused to take.

Boston-based U.S. District Judge Richard Stearns gave the administration two weeks to enable the return of Any Lucia Lopez Belloza, a student from Babson College in Massachusetts who was deported after being detained at Boston's Logan Airport while traveling to spend the Thanksgiving holiday with her family in Texas.

"Wisdom counsels that redemption may be found by acknowledging and fixing our own errors," Stearns wrote. "In this unfortunate case, the government commendably admits that it did wrong. Now it is time for the government to make amends."

Stearns, who was appointed by Democratic President Bill Clinton, said he had hoped to avoid holding anyone in civil contempt by giving the administration a chance to correct what it called a "mistake."

But the State Department last week called the judge's recommendation to issue her a new student visa "unfeasible," and U.S. Immigration and Customs Enforcement declined to facilitate her return, prompting Friday's order.

The U.S. Department of Homeland Security, which includes ICE, and Lopez Belloza's lawyer did not immediately respond to requests for comment.

The 20-year-old college freshman is a Honduran national who was brought to the U.S. by her mother, who was seeking asylum, when she was 8 years old. Babson is located in Wellesley, Massachusetts.

Lopez Belloza has said she was unaware she was subject to a final order of removal, which was the basis for her arrest.

She was flown to Honduras on November 22 even though her lawyer had secured a court order in Massachusetts on the previous day barring Lopez Belloza from being deported or transferred out of the state for 72 hours. She remains in Honduras with her grandparents.

A lawyer for the government at a hearing last month apologized for the violation of the court's order, attributing it to a "mistake" by an ICE officer who did not properly flag it.

data.title: Apax Partners walks away from Pinewood deal, citing tough market conditions
data.description:
Private equity firm Apax Partners withdrew its offer for British automotive service provider Pinewood Technologies > on Friday, citing prevailing challenging market conditions.

Apax Partners had proposed to buy Pinewood Technologies in a $792 million deal in late January, which had sent the shares of the London-listed firm up nearly 30%.

On January 29, Pinewood Technologies said that it was in discussions to finalize a 500 pence per share offer from the private equity firm, if a formal offer was tabled on or before February 26. The company, which offers a cloud-based platform for car dealerships and manufacturers, had already received multiple takeover bids from London-based Apax Partners.

Apax did not specify what challenging market conditions made it walk away from its offer, while Pinewood did not comment on Apax's statement immediately when contacted by Reuters.
